name: Week 10 KB Conditioning — Skill Drill + Mobility (Light, Form-First)
description: Thursday is the lightest day of the program — by design. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs. Drill Tuesday's cues at low load, refine what was clunky, groove what clicked. **Spotlight: bent press** (lateral hinge mechanics — the leverage cue from W7 takes weeks to make automatic). 20 min, zero leg loading, no swings, no C&P, no goblet squats. Heaviest bell touched: 25lb. Should feel effortless. Ends with a hip-and-adductor cooldown — hips get tight on KB days.

dayOverview.theme: Thursday is recovery + skill drilling, NOT a workout. Drill Tuesday's cues at low load. Should leave you fresher, not more cooked.

spotlights.why: Bent press is mechanically the most technique-dense KB lift — lateral hinge + overhead load + eye-track + leverage press. The W7 click ('groove the path') needs weeks of light reps to become automatic. Drill it here at 18-20lb where the brain can pay attention to the cues, not the load.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 bent press · W11 windmill mechanics · W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 cycle back. Thursday is always lighter than Tuesday's spotlight.
watchpoints: **Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges. Legs day is tomorrow.
If anything feels heavy, the load is wrong. Pull weight or skip the block.
Boring is the goal. Don't innovate on light skill day — same blueprint as W7-W8 with cleaner cues.
5 hard days/week = injury. Thursday MUST stay light, even if you feel fresh.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Slow circles, both directions. Shoulder loosener — that's it.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (Scap Activation, ~3 min)

exercises.notes: Heels tucked, mini-band overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension** — primes scap upward rotation for bent press + TGU.
Pull band down behind head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress.
Band wraps the upper back. **Press out to T** — fires rear delts and mid-traps.
Band at sternum, **pull apart, scaps retract**, ribs down.
Sweep arms further back. **Don't overarch** the lower back.
15s each side. Half-windmill with band — **primes the bent press lateral hinge cue** (today's spotlight).
Both arms in V/Y overhead. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Scap + rear delt activation before the bent press spotlight + skill work. Sustained tension, no rest between positions.

sets.name: OTD Flow (Light Groove)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light and smooth.
- Bell starts on the floor between feet
- **Hips drive the lift**, not the arm
- Catch in rack with **knees slightly bent** (Tuesday's spotlight cue)
Goblet/rack position. **Sit tall**, full depth, no bouncing. Same depth as gym squats — find the floor of the squat.
Stride out wide, **sit into the working leg**, keep the other leg long. Working leg's hip in external rotation. Mobility, not strength.
**Tuesday's spotlight cue** — see Skill Drilling block below for the full pattern. Light groove here.
**Control on the way down** — bell to floor with the same cleanness as the way up. The tactical clean's whole point is the controlled descent.

sets.notes: 20lb, 2 reps each side x 2 rounds. Should feel effortless throughout.

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 18-20lb (light to keep the cues clean, NOT 25). 2 rounds (not 3 — keep it skill-day light).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Bell starts in rack, eye on bell throughout** — the head turn IS part of the lift
- **Lateral hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — push the hip out sideways, knee soft on bell side
- **Spine stays long** — this is a *lateral hinge with overhead load*, NOT a side bend. If you feel the lower back compress, you're side-bending.
- **Press up by moving body DOWN, not by pushing bell UP** — the press is mostly leverage. The bell barely moves; your body changes shape under it.
- Slow descent, controlled return to rack — no slam
- **Free hand:** can drift to inside of front foot for counterbalance, but the bell-side stays vertical

**Reflection:** does the lateral hinge feel like a hinge, or are you side-bending? Side-bending = lower back load. Hinging = hip + glute load.

**LOAD:** 18-20lb. Light. Same hinge family as bent press.
- Bell locked overhead, **eye tracks the bell**
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — same pattern as bent press
- Reach free hand toward inside of front foot
- **Long spine** — stretch should land in the **hip flexor of the back leg** and the hamstrings, NOT the lower back

**LOAD:** 25lb (max bell of the day). Lighter intent than Tuesday — drill the **left side** transitions (still the weak link).
- Pause at every checkpoint: lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand
- Reverse with the same discipline — the descent is half the lift
- Left side first, get the freshest reps to the weak side
60s each side. Hip flexor release — KB flow + cossack + lateral lunge fries the hip flexors. This counters.
60s. Adductors get tight from B-stance, cossack squats, and lateral lunges. Sit, soles together, hinge forward, breathe.

tips: **THURSDAY = SKILL + MOBILITY, NOT WORKOUT.** ~23 min, light. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs.
**NEW W10:** Seated mini-band shoulder prep added after the halo warmup (@sculptherbody flow). 7 positions, ~3 min, sustained tension. Primes the bent press lateral hinge spotlight.
**Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges.
Heaviest bell: 25lb (TGU only). Most of the session at 18-20lb.
Spotlight: bent press lateral hinge. Drill the leverage cue at light load — automaticity is the goal.
Skill maintenance + Tuesday's cue refinement. If a cue from Tuesday felt off, this is when you fix it (low-stakes reps).
Cooldown is mandatory — pigeon + butterfly. Hips and adductors get tight on KB days.
Should feel effortless. If anything feels like work, you've gone too hard. Pull back.
formPhilosophy: Light grooves are how cues become automatic. Bent press leverage takes weeks of low-load reps before the body 'gets it.' Same with TGU left-side stability. This is the day where complex skill becomes default movement — without ego, without load, without rush.
